title: Paying Cash — Expense vs Payable
event: Reyes Co. writes a check. Prepare the entry.

lines.label: this period's cost
lines.why: The space was used THIS period — that's what makes it this period's expense.
lines.trap: If the payment covered FUTURE months, it would be Prepaid Rent (an asset), not expense.

lines.label: paid
lines.trap: Same credit both variants — the entire question lives on the debit side.

entries.label: Settling an old promise — no new expense

lines.label: liability settled
lines.why: Paying off the liability recorded when the bill first arrived.
lines.trap: NO expense here. The expense (or asset) was recorded when the bill was incurred — this is just settling up.

lines.label: paid
lines.trap: Cash out ≠ expense. The expense's timing was set when the cost was INCURRED.

memorize.body: When cash goes out, ask: NEW cost of this period (expense) or paying an OLD promise (payable)? Same credit, totally different debit.
Cash going out and expense being recognized are separate events that only sometimes coincide.

questions.prompt: Reyes pays $750 on its account payable. Expense recognized in this entry = ?

distractors.feedback: Paying a bill isn't the expense — incurring it was. Debit the liability, not an expense account.

build.scaffold: Credit Cash is a given. The whole question is the debit: new cost → expense; old bill → payable.
